data.content: Starting `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically on file changes:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText: The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` to get hot reload for their tests. This is an MSTest project targeting .NET 8.0. I'll start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so they can see the tests re-run automatically when they make changes.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-2b1cdd83f35c42acbdefd65cf92d2bfe && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is running. It found a build error — `ArgumentException` needs the `System` namespace. The watcher is waiting for you to save a fix, then it will re-run automatically.

**Current failure:** `CS0246` — missing `using System;` in `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs` (line 25 uses `ArgumentException`).

Also note the express test will fail: it expects `15.00` but `CalculateShipping` returns `10.00` for `"express"`.

The watcher is live — just edit your file and save, and tests will re-run. Want me to fix those issues for you?
